text:
Market today: Tech is getting hit again - AI names specifically are under pressure, and OpenAI's IPO delay is muddying the narrative that's been propping up high-multiple names like yours. This isn't a calm dip; it's sentiment-driven selling in exactly your lane.

Your holdings: PLTR is the sharpest pain - down 17.56% and you're on margin, so that's a real dollar hole. SELL-rated, watch the $85 stop closely. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18% and flagged TRIM - on margin, a big slot in a sliding name is a compounding problem. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - limited data means no clean read; margin amplifies any gap-down hard.

Watch: 1) Whether AI stock selling accelerates into Friday's massive volume/quadruple-witching-style event - that could flush names like PLTR and AVGO lower fast. 2) Your AVGO stop at $336 - largest position, closest thing to a landmine if today's mood worsens.

Bottom line: Margin plus three red flags in a tech selloff day means your only job today is protecting capital, not finding new trades.
